title: Gently Sweet French Rice Canele
description: A traditional Basque-French dessert featuring crispy edges and a soft center, made with light brown soft sugar and fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, this canele is a unique and adventurous treat. It is also Buddhist-temple-friendly.

ingredients.name: fry-grind-rice-khao-khua
ingredients.notes: also known as toasted rice powder

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the sugar, fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, and salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and vanilla extract.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and whisk until just combined.
Melt the butter and let it cool slightly.
Add the melted butter to the egg mixture and whisk until smooth.
Pour the batter into canele molds and bake for 25 minutes.
Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the caneles from the oven and let them cool in the molds for 5 minutes.
Remove the caneles from the molds and transfer them to a wire rack to cool completely.
